description:
# Why

bl-f37b made `bl show --resolve-remote` route through `delivered_repo`
when the local tag scan misses. The parallel gap on the *write* side
remains: `delivery::populate_on_close` still calls the local-only
`resolve(repo_root, target_branch, task)` (see src/delivery.rs:170 on
main 86bf8f7), so a `bl close` running on a hub or sibling clone whose
history doesn't carry the `[bl-xxxx]` squash silently archives the task
with `delivered_in: null` — even though `delivered_repo` is sitting
right there in the task file and *would* resolve via the bl-f37b
machinery.

This matters most for the bridging pattern (README §Bridging): the
bridge clone's `sync` hook closes a task that landed in repo B, and the
bridge clone has no copy of B's main. Without remote resolution on
close, the archived task loses its sha provenance. The bl-733e
`--delivered-repo` override patches part of this for the operator who
already knows the sha, but the unattended-sync case still drops it.

# Scope

- `populate_on_close`: when the hint-and-local-scan miss and the task
  has `delivered_repo` set, opt into `resolve_with(..., ResolveOpts {
  remote: true })` (or an equivalent call to `delivery_remote::resolve`).
- New opt-in surface: either a `bl close --resolve-remote` flag mirroring
  `bl show --resolve-remote`, a config knob, or an internal default
  routed through the sync path (since sync hooks are the primary
  driver). Probably the flag — the bridge can opt in from its hook and
  human operators stay quiet.
- Tests: hub or sibling clone whose state branch knows a delivered_repo
  pointing at a fetchable URL with the `[bl-xxxx]` commit. Confirm
  `bl close` writes the sha into the archived task. Soft-fail when the
  URL is unreachable — same warn-once policy as the show path.

# Open questions

- Whether to also auto-engage on the deferred-mode `bl close` path
  (where the gate child closes a delivery the closer has never seen).
  Probably yes — the deferred-mode `bl close` is exactly the case where
  the local clone may not be the one that produced the squash.
- Whether to expose the resolution outcome (e.g. a note on the close
  commit) or just write it silently.

# Origin

Bl-f37b review pass — flagged as the symmetric gap to the read-side
resolver and deferred so the bl-f37b ball stayed scoped to `bl show`.
title: bl close: resolve delivered_in via delivered_repo on local miss
